CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-06-2021 Set For Trial On Nov-15-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Matthew Ebrahimoons Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-15-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. LARISSA A. BRANES, Esq. of Rudderow Law Group (Tel: 949-565-1344) for Plaintiff Calculated Risk Analytics LLC dba Excelerate Capital;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF CALCULATED RISK ANALYTICS LLC's Motion To Compel Defendant Matthew Ebrahimoons Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for plaintiff authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion of Calculated Risk Analytics LLC ("CRA") is GRANTED. Matthew Ebrahimoon's objections contained in his responses to CRA's Special Interrogatories (Set One) are overruled except as to information that is actually privileged from disclosure, and he must provide further responses. Mr. Ebrahimoon's answers must be as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available to him permits. CCP Sec. 2030.220(a), (b). If an interrogatory cannot be answered completely, it shall be answered to the extent possible. Id. Mr. Ebrahimoon must state the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth in answering CRA's interrogatories. Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Const. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 76; see CCP Sec. 2023.010(f) (evasive response is ground for sanctions). In those instances where Mr. Ebrahimoon asserts privilege in the course of responding to interrogatories that request him to identify documents, he must provide a description of the documents sufficient to permit evaluation of the privilege claims. (Hernandez v. Sup. Ct. (Acheson, Indus. Inc.) (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 285, 293.)

Mr. Ebrahimoon shall serve verified code-compliant further responses to the interrogatories by February 5, 2021. The term "code-compliant" means in compliance with the California Civil Discovery Act, Code of Civil Procedure sections 2016.010 et seq., as interpreted by the decisions of the courts of this state, including, without limitation, the case decisions cited in CRA's moving papers.

CRA's request for monetary sanctions against Mr. Ebrahimoon and his counsel of record is GRANTED in the amount of $1,569.38, said sum to be paid by February 5, 2021.

Plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at pvestal@nvlawllp.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

The Motion of Calculated Risk Analytics LLC ("CRA") is GRANTED. APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. LARISSA A. BRANES, Esq. of Rudderow Law Group (Tel: 949-565-1344) for Plaintiff Calculated Risk Analytics LLC dba Excelerate Capital;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF CALCULATED RISK ANALYTICS LLC's Motion To Compel Defendant Matthew Ebrahimoons Further Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Plaintiff stipulates to the matter decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for plaintiff authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion of Calculated Risk Analytics LLC ("CRA") is GRANTED. Matthew Ebrahimoon's objections contained in his responses to CRA's Form Interrogatories (Set One) are overruled except as to information that is actually privileged from disclosure, and he must provide further responses. The responses to interrogatories Nos. 50.1, 50.2, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5 and 50.6 shall be without objections including ones based on privilege or the protection of attorney work product.

Mr. Ebrahimoon's answers must be as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available to him permits. CCP Sec. 2030.220(a), (b). If an interrogatory cannot be answered completely, it shall be answered to the extent possible. Id. Mr. Ebrahimoon must state the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth in answering CRA's interrogatories. Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Const. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 76; see CCP Sec. 2023.010(f) (evasive response is ground for sanctions). In those instances where Mr. Ebrahimoon asserts privilege in the course of responding to interrogatories that request him to identify documents, he must provide a description of the documents sufficient to permit evaluation of the privilege claims. (Hernandez v. Sup. Ct. (Acheson, Indus. Inc.) (2003) 112 Cal.App.4th 285, 293.)

Mr. Ebrahimoon shall serve verified code-compliant further responses to the interrogatories by February 5, 2021. The term "code-compliant" means in compliance with the California Civil Discovery Act, Code of Civil Procedure sections 2016.010 et seq., as interpreted by the decisions of the courts of this state, including, without limitation, the case decisions cited in CRA's moving papers.

CRA's request for monetary sanctions against Mr. Ebrahimoon and his counsel of record is GRANTED in the amount of $1,569.38, said sum to be paid by February 5, 2021.

Plaintiff to prepare and submit a proposed order to the Judge Pro Tem at pvestal@nvlawllp.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Vestal; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
